meta.title: Nishikawa Town Ski Area
meta.description: Gateway to the Gassan area—minutes from Nishikawa IC, hub for regional onsen, food, and tours.

hero.title:
Nishikawa Town
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Gateway to Mt. Gassan—minutes from Nishikawa IC, your regional hub.

triangle.title:
Winter town hill,
spring link to Gassan.
triangle.lead: Nishikawa Town Ski Area is the gateway to the Gassan region. Three compact courses and nightly skiing serve locals, while Mizusawa Onsenkan and Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa anchor loops to Yamagata city dining and spring skiing on Mt. Gassan. Gassan Tourism Development operates this hill in winter and Gassan Ski Area in spring and summer—one corridor across seasons.
triangle.noticeBody: Gassan Ski Area runs Apr–Jul for spring skiing only. Winter stays center on nightly skiing here plus nearby onsen; once Route 112 snow walls melt, shift base to Gassan Resort Inn for spring runs—the core rhythm of the Gassan Corridor.
triangle.imgAlt: Gassan-area snowscape and slopes—the regional hub

primary.title: 5 min from Nishikawa IC, nightly town hill
primary.lead: One pair lift, three courses, 320 m vertical. Day ticket ¥2,100 adults · ¥1,000 children, 150 free parking spaces. Easy loops from after-work night skiing to wider regional travel at the Gassan gateway.
primary.imgAlt: Lit night slopes—Nishikawa Town Ski Area

secondary.title: 5 minutes to Mizusawa Onsenkan
secondary.lead: Mizusawa Onsenkan beside Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa and Umiami Onsen Unagi-yu—fold post-ski recovery and local hot-spring culture into one Nishikawa IC–adjacent hub.

mobility.title:
Regional routing
and seasons.
mobility.lead: ~30 minutes from Yamagata city, ~5 minutes from Nishikawa IC. Rent at Shinjo Station and loop the Gassan Corridor—onsen, food, and spring-ski bases—with less winter-driving stress.

jozankei.route: Mizusawa Onsenkan ⇔ Nishikawa Town Ski Area

sapporo.route: Central Yamagata ⇔ Nishikawa Town Ski Area

otaru.route: Gassan Ski Area (spring) ⇔ Nishikawa Town Ski Area

airport.route: Nishikawa IC ⇔ Nishikawa Town Ski Area

mobility.rentacarLink: Compare rental cars at Shinjo Station
mobility.imgAlt: Route 112 and the Gassan range—regional travel corridor

picks.title:
Curated
shortlist.
picks.lead: Stays, onsen, and dining chosen on five axes—snowscape, local food, onsen comfort, lodging, and access. A list you can build a Gassan Corridor loop from.

chaletIvy.name: Gassan Resort Inn
chaletIvy.hook: Closest stay to Gassan Ski Area—spring-ski and hiking base. Operated by Gassan Tourism Development.

kuriyasuizan.name: Minshuku Hashimotoso
kuriyasuizan.hook: Inn near Oisawa Onsen Yuttari-kan. Pair 100% spring-water baths with an overnight Gassan Corridor base.

yurakusoan.name: Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa
yurakusoan.hook: Gassan spring water and an on-site brewery. Restaurant plus Mizusawa Onsenkan for a stay-style hub visit.

unwind.name: Hotel Metropolitan Yamagata
unwind.hook: Directly at JR Yamagata Station—urban sightseeing linked to day trips to the slopes.

omo5.name: Yamagata Grand Hotel
omo5.hook: Downtown business and tourism hotel—~30 minutes to Nishikawa IC from central Yamagata.

hoheikyo.name: Mizusawa Onsenkan
hoheikyo.hook: Gassan and Yakushi baths plus Finnish sauna "Sauna Gassan"—next to Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa.

koganeyu.name: Umiami Onsen Unagi-yu
koganeyu.hook: Nishikawa's first spring—rich, silky water at a community bath rooted in the town.

garaku.name: Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa · Gassan sansai soba
garaku.hook: Local wild-plant soba—cold noodles dipped in hot broth, finished Nishikawa-style with hatto.

masazushi.name: Midoritei
masazushi.hook: Chinese-style noodles since 1930. Spicy miso and natto ramen are local soul food.

biergarten.name: Gassan Beer (Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa)
biergarten.hook: Yamagata's only roadside-station brewery—craft beer with a view of the tanks.

amenoyuki.name: Beer soft-serve
amenoyuki.hook: Soft-serve with Gassan Beer wort—a sweet finish after skiing.

food.label: Dining
food.title: 4 curated restaurants
food.hook: Sansai soba, niboshi ramen, local beer—food that drives circulation.

tour.title:
Night skiing in winter,
Gassan in spring.
tour.lead: Nightly runs at the IC-adjacent town hill → recovery at Mizusawa Onsenkan → Yamagata city fare—as seasons shift, connect via Gassan Resort Inn to spring skiing on Mt. Gassan.

02.title: Mizusawa Onsenkan
02.body: ~5 min from the hill. Sauna Gassan recovery

03.title: Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa
03.body: Gassan sansai soba and beer soft-serve

04.title: Gassan Resort Inn
04.body: Pre-spring-ski base—steps from Gassan Ski Area

05.title: Gassan Ski Area (spring)
05.body: Apr–Jul spring skiing—one of Japan's longest seasons

triangle.title: Complete Gassan Corridor guide
triangle.body: Use Nishikawa Town Ski Area as the hub—onsen, food, Yamagata city, and spring Gassan skiing in one journey. Gassan Tourism Development links winter and spring hills by season.

mobility.title: Seasonal routes and IC access
mobility.body: Winter centers on Nishikawa IC night skiing. Spring opens Route 112 snow corridors toward Gassan—~30 min from Yamagata city, rental loops from Shinjo Station, schedules matched to the season.

culture.title: Local food and baths—raising guest standards
culture.body: Michi-no-Eki local menus, Mizusawa sauna, Midoritei's ramen variety—lift individual venue efforts to area norms. Multilingual visibility at booking is the competitiveness lever.

access.note: ~5 min by car from Nishikawa IC on the Yamagata Expressway · ~30 min from central Yamagata · 150 free parking spaces

garaku.title: Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa · Niboshi Chuka Soba
garaku.lead: Mikuro-supervised niboshi broth—restaurant-grade ramen at a roadside station.

beer-garden.title: Gassan Beer (Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa)
beer-garden.lead: Yamagata's only roadside-station brewery—fresh craft beer with local bites.

ame-yuki.title: Michi-no-Eki Nishikawa · Gassan sansai soba
ame-yuki.lead: Wild plants and mushrooms in hot dipping broth—finish Nishikawa-style with hatto.

chalet-ivy.lead: Closest lodging to Gassan Ski Area—spring skiing and hiking base. Operated by Gassan Tourism Development.

kuriyasuizan.title: Minshuku Hashimotoso
kuriyasuizan.lead: Near Oisawa Onsen Yuttari-kan—cedar interiors and source-fed baths in quiet mountain country.
